content.content:
1→---
     2→- name: Populate OpenRewrite Recipes in Consul KV
     3→  hosts: linux_hosts
     4→  become: true
     5→  
     6→  vars:
     7→    consul_url: "http://localhost:8500"
     8→    recipe_base_path: "ploy/arf/recipes"
     9→    
    10→    openrewrite_recipes:
    11→      # Java Migration recipes
    12→      - id: "openrewrite-java11to17"
    13→        name: "java11to17"
    14→        description: "Migrate Java 11 to Java 17"
    15→        categories: ["java-migration"]
    16→        tags: ["java", "migration", "openrewrite", "java17"]
    17→        recipe_class: "org.openrewrite.java.migrate.Java11toJava17"
    18→        artifact_id: "rewrite-migrate-java"
    19→        group_id: "org.openrewrite.recipe"
    20→        version: "2.11.0"
    21→      
    22→      - id: "openrewrite-java8to11"
    23→        name: "java8to11"
    24→        description: "Migrate Java 8 to Java 11"
    25→        categories: ["java-migration"]
    26→        tags: ["java", "migration", "openrewrite", "java11"]
    27→        recipe_class: "org.openrewrite.java.migrate.Java8toJava11"
    28→        artifact_id: "rewrite-migrate-java"
    29→        group_id: "org.openrewrite.recipe"
    30→        version: "2.11.0"
    31→        
    32→      - id: "openrewrite-jakarta"
    33→        name: "jakarta"
    34→        description: "Migrate from javax to Jakarta EE"
    35→        categories: ["java-migration"]
    36→        tags: ["java", "migration", "openrewrite", "jakarta", "javax"]
    37→        recipe_class: "org.openrewrite.java.migrate.jakarta.JavaxMigrationToJakarta"
    38→        artifact_id: "rewrite-migrate-java"
    39→        group_id: "org.openrewrite.recipe"
    40→        version: "2.11.0"
    41→      
    42→      # Spring recipes
    43→      - id: "openrewrite-spring-boot-3"
    44→        name: "spring-boot-3"
    45→        description: "Upgrade to Spring Boot 3.2"
    46→        categories: ["spring"]
    47→        tags: ["spring", "spring-boot", "openrewrite", "upgrade"]
    48→        recipe_class: "org.openrewrite.java.spring.boot3.UpgradeSpringBoot_3_2"
    49→        artifact_id: "rewrite-spring"
    50→        group_id: "org.openrewrite.recipe"
    51→        version: "5.7.0"
    52→        
    53→      - id: "openrewrite-spring-security-6"
    54→        name: "spring-security-6"
    55→        description: "Upgrade to Spring Security 6.0"
    56→        categories: ["spring"]
    57→        tags: ["spring", "security", "openrewrite", "upgrade"]
    58→        recipe_class: "org.openrewrite.java.spring.security6.UpgradeSpringSecurity_6_0"
    59→        artifact_id: "rewrite-spring"
    60→        group_id: "org.openrewrite.recipe"
    61→        version: "5.7.0"
    62→      
    63→      # Testing recipes
    64→      - id: "openrewrite-junit5"
    65→        name: "junit5"
    66→        description: "Migrate from JUnit 4 to JUnit 5"
    67→        categories: ["testing"]
    68→        tags: ["testing", "junit", "openrewrite", "migration"]
    69→        recipe_class: "org.openrewrite.java.testing.junit5.JUnit4to5Migration"
    70→        artifact_id: "rewrite-testing-frameworks"
    71→        group_id: "org.openrewrite.recipe"
    72→        version: "2.6.0"
    73→        
    74→      - id: "openrewrite-mockito"
    75→        name: "mockito"
    76→        description: "Migrate Mockito 1 to Mockito 4"
    77→        categories: ["testing"]
    78→        tags: ["testing", "mockito", "openrewrite", "migration"]
    79→        recipe_class: "org.openrewrite.java.testing.mockito.Mockito1to4Migration"
    80→        artifact_id: "rewrite-testing-frameworks"
    81→        group_id: "org.openrewrite.recipe"
    82→        version: "2.6.0"
    83→        
    84→      - id: "openrewrite-assertj"
    85→        name: "assertj"
    86→        description: "Convert JUnit assertions to AssertJ"
    87→        categories: ["testing"]
    88→        tags: ["testing", "assertj", "openrewrite", "junit"]
    89→        recipe_class: "org.openrewrite.java.testing.assertj.JUnitToAssertj"
    90→        artifact_id: "rewrite-testing-frameworks"
    91→        group_id: "org.openrewrite.recipe"
    92→        version: "2.6.0"
    93→      
    94→      # Logging recipes
    95→      - id: "openrewrite-slf4j"
    96→        name: "slf4j"
    97→        description: "Apply SLF4J best practices"
    98→        categories: ["logging"]
    99→        tags: ["logging", "slf4j", "openrewrite", "best-practices"]
   100→        recipe_class: "org.openrewrite.java.logging.slf4j.Slf4jBestPractices"
   101→        artifact_id: "rewrite-logging-frameworks"
   102→        group_id: "org.openrewrite.recipe"
   103→        version: "2.5.0"
   104→        
   105→      - id: "openrewrite-log4j2"
   106→        name: "log4j2"
   107→        description: "Migrate from Log4j 1.x to Log4j 2.x"
   108→        categories: ["logging"]
   109→        tags: ["logging", "log4j", "openrewrite", "migration"]
   110→        recipe_class: "org.openrewrite.java.logging.log4j.Log4j1ToLog4j2"
   111→        artifact_id: "rewrite-logging-frameworks"
   112→        group_id: "org.openrewrite.recipe"
   113→        version: "2.5.0"
   114→
   115→  tasks:
   116→    - name: Wait for Consul to be ready
   117→      wait_for:
   118→        port: 8500
   119→        host: localhost
   120→        delay: 5
   121→        timeout: 60
   122→      
   123→    - name: Check Consul health
   124→      uri:
   125→        url: "{{ consul_url }}/v1/status/leader"
   126→        method: GET
   127→        status_code: 200
   128→      register: consul_health
   129→      retries: 5
   130→      delay: 2
   131→      until: consul_health.status == 200
   132→
   133→    - name: Store OpenRewrite recipes in Consul KV
   134→      uri:
   135→        url: "{{ consul_url }}/v1/kv/{{ recipe_base_path }}/{{ item.id }}"
   136→        method: PUT
   137→        body: |
   138→          {
   139→            "id": "{{ item.id }}",
   140→            "metadata": {
   141→              "name": "{{ item.name }}",
   142→              "description": "{{ item.description }}",
   143→              "categories": {{ item.categories | to_json }},
   144→              "tags": {{ item.tags | to_json }},
   145→              "author": "Ploy Platform",
   146→              "version": "1.0.0"
   147→            },
   148→            "steps": [{
   149→              "name": "Apply OpenRewrite Recipe",
   150→              "type": "openrewrite",
   151→              "config": {
   152→                "recipe": "{{ item.recipe_class }}",
   153→                "artifactId": "{{ item.artifact_id }}",
   154→                "groupId": "{{ item.group_id }}",
   155→                "version": "{{ item.version }}"
   156→              }
   157→            }],
   158→            "execution": {
   159→              "strategy": "sequential",
   160→              "retry_policy": {
   161→                "max_attempts": 3,
   162→                "backoff": "exponential"
   163→              }
   164→            },
   165→            "created_at": "{{ ansible_date_time.iso8601 }}",
   166→            "updated_at": "{{ ansible_date_time.iso8601 }}"
   167→          }
   168→        body_format: json
   169→        status_code: [200, 201]
   170→      loop: "{{ openrewrite_recipes }}"
   171→      loop_control:
   172→        label: "{{ item.name }}"
   173→      
   174→    - name: Verify recipes were stored
   175→      uri:
   176→        url: "{{ consul_url }}/v1/kv/{{ recipe_base_path }}?keys"
   177→        method: GET
   178→        status_code: 200
   179→      register: stored_recipes
   180→      
   181→    - name: Display stored recipe keys
   182→      debug:
   183→        msg: "Successfully stored {{ stored_recipes.json | length }} OpenRewrite recipes in Consul"
   184→        
   185→    - name: Create recipe index for quick lookup
   186→      uri:
   187→        url: "{{ consul_url }}/v1/kv/{{ recipe_base_path }}/index/openrewrite"
   188→        method: PUT
   189→        body: |
   190→          {
   191→            "recipes": {{ openrewrite_recipes | map(attribute='name') | list | to_json }},
   192→            "last_updated": "{{ ansible_date_time.iso8601 }}"
   193→          }
   194→        body_format: raw
   195→        headers:
   196→          Content-Type: "application/json"
   197→        status_code: [200, 201]

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it looks malicious. If it does, you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer high-level questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.thinking:
I can see there are two playbooks:
1. `arf-openrewrite-recipes-registry.yml` - The new one for RecipeRegistry, uses `/v1/arf/recipes/register` endpoint
2. `arf-openrewrite-recipes.yml` - The old one for Consul KV directly

The new playbook is targeting the RecipeRegistry system, but I need to check if the `/v1/arf/recipes/register` endpoint exists in our handlers. Let me search for this endpoint.
Let me check if there's a register endpoint in the handler routes.
Great! I can see there's a `RegisterRecipeFromRunner` handler for the `/recipes/register` endpoint. Now I need to check if this handler exists and if it's compatible with the RecipeRegistry system.
Perfect! Let me check the handler_register.go file to see the implementation of RegisterRecipeFromRunner and verify it's working with the new RecipeRegistry system.

I can see several issues with the RegisterRecipeFromRunner handler:

1. **Lines 44-67**: There's a fallback that returns success even when the recipeRegistry is nil. This means the endpoint will appear to work but won't actually register recipes.

2. **Lines 57-58**: There's a TODO comment indicating the recipe registry initialization is incomplete.

The problem is that the handler is designed to work but has incomplete initialization logic. The Ansible playbook is calling this endpoint expecting it to work, but it's not actually registering the recipes properly when the recipeRegistry is not initialized.

I need to:
1. Fix the RegisterRecipeFromRunner handler to ensure it properly initializes or reports errors
2. Test the Ansible playbook
3. Make sure the initialization works correctly

Let me first check if there are any other issues and then fix the handler.

content.content: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable

content.thinking:
I found the issue! The `RegisterRecipeFromRunner` handler has incomplete initialization logic. When `h.recipeRegistry` is nil, it just logs and returns a success response without actually registering the recipe. This means the Ansible playbook will think it's working, but no recipes are actually being stored.

Let me fix this by:
1. Ensuring the handler properly reports when RecipeRegistry is not available
2. Making sure it fails gracefully when storage isn't available
3. Removing the fallback that pretends registration worked
